title: Cluster-Aware Updating (CAU) Run Status, Node Drain Success, and Patch-Window Compliance
exclusions: Standalone Windows Server hosts without Failover-Clustering (CAU is cluster-only); third-party patch-orchestration tools (SCCM software-update-groups, Tanium, BigFix) — separate UC; Linux package-manager rolling updates (yum/dnf/apt — different patch model); Hyper-V VM guest-OS patching (handled by guest WSUS/SCCM, not CAU); Microsoft Update Catalog manual KB downloads outside CAU (separate audit trail).
dataSources: `index=patching` (mirrored `windows`, `wineventlog`, `azure_stack_hci` per naming) blending **`Splunk_TA_windows`** (**Splunkbase** [**742**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) **WinEventLog** inputs on **`Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin`** (IDs **1001** CAU run started, **1002** completed successfully, **1003** failed, **1006** node update started, **1007** node update completed, **1008** node update failed with **`failure_reason`** / **`error_id`**, **1011**/**1012** **`PreUpdateScript`**, **1015**/**1016** **`PostUpdateScript`**, **1023** **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`** timeout exceeded, **1027** **`Resume-ClusterNode`** fault after restart) plus **`Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating-Management/Admin`** (**Add-CauClusterRole**, **`Set-CauClusterRole`** lifecycle). Scripted **HEC** **`cau:report`** from **`Get-CauReport -ClusterName <cluster> -Last N`** (**`Summary`**, **`NodeUpdateState[]`**, **`WorkflowConfiguration`**, **`RunPluginConfigs`**); **`cau:run`** snapshots from **`Get-CauRun`** (**`RunId`**, **`Status`**, **`CurrentNodeName`**, **`NodeStatusNotifications`** with **`Idle`/`Searching`/`Downloading`/`Staging`/`Installing`/`Restarting`/`WaitingForRestart`/`Verifying`/`Failed`/`Succeeded`**, **`UpdateTitle`**, **`RunPlugin`**). Optional **`cau:plugin`** from **`Get-CauPlugin`** (**`Microsoft.WindowsUpdatePlugin`** vs **`Microsoft.HotfixPlugin`** SMB **`\\share\\kb`** paths). **`Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational`** optional joins for **`Suspend`/`Resume`** corroboration.
app: **`Splunk_TA_windows`** ([Splunkbase **742**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) on cluster nodes or aggregating Heavy Forwarders; optional **`Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure`** where **`Azure Stack HCI`** ARM telemetry complements **`CAU`** timelines.
spl:
(index=patching OR index=windows OR index=wineventlog OR index=azure_stack_hci)
(sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin" OR sourcetype="cau:report" OR sourcetype="cau:run")
earliest=-24h
| eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventCode,event_id,EventID,""))
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<fail_blob>failure_reason|FailureReason)[=:]\s*(?<reason_tail>[^\|\r\n]+)"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<err_tail>error_id|ErrorId)[=:]\s*(?<err_code>[^\|\r\n]+)"
| eval run_status=upper(trim(coalesce(Status,"")))
| eval json_Status=upper(trim(coalesce(Summary_Status,report_Status,"")))
| eval combined_status=coalesce(run_status,json_Status)
| eval plugin_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(RunPlugin,run_plugin,"")))
| eval urgent=case(
    evt IN ("1003","1008","1023","1027"), 3,
    combined_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED"), 3,
    run_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED"), 3,
    match(lower(_raw), "(?i)suspend-clusternode\s+-drain.*timeout"), 3,
    match(lower(_raw), "(?i)resume-clusternode.*fail"), 3,
    true(),1)
| where urgent=3 OR combined_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED") OR (evt IN ("1011","1012","1015","1016") AND match(lower(_raw),"fail"))
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(ClusterName,cluster_name,fq_cluster,ComputerName)))
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(evt) as last_evt,
       latest(combined_status) as report_status,
       latest(CurrentNodeName) as current_node,
       latest(RunId) as run_id,
       values(plugin_hint) as plugins_seen,
       values(reason_tail) as failure_hints,
       values(err_code) as error_hints,
       dc(host) as reporting_hosts
  by cluster_key
| eval posture=case(report_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED") OR last_evt IN ("1003","1008","1023","1027"),"immediate_review",true(),"monitor")
| sort -last_seen
| table cluster_key, posture, last_evt, report_status, current_node, run_id, plugins_seen, failure_hints, error_hints, reporting_hosts, last_seen
description: Tracks **Cluster-Aware Updating** orchestration across **Failover Cluster** nodes—correlating **`Get-CauRun`**/**`Get-CauReport`** cmdlet snapshots (**`RunId`**, **`Status`**, **`NodeStatusNotifications`**, **`Summary`**) with **`Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin`** events (**1003**, **1008**, **1023**, **1027**) plus **`PreUpdateScript`**/**`PostUpdateScript`** outcomes—to expose **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`** stalls (**GPU-passthrough**, **anti-affinity**, **snapshot merge**, **clustered IP** dependencies), **`Microsoft.WindowsUpdatePlugin`** versus **`Microsoft.HotfixPlugin`** chain faults, and patch-window adherence before clustered roles drift across uneven **`KB`** states.
value: Patch orchestration faults strand clustered VMs behind **`Drain`** timeouts, split **`KB`** inventories across nodes, or silently abort **`Invoke-CauRun`** waves—risking asymmetric vulnerabilities and brittle failover math. Folding **`CAU`** cmdlet feeds next to authoritative **`WinEventLog`** channels lets operators pinpoint whether **`Microsoft.Update`** **`plug-ins`**, **`Resume-ClusterNode`** automation, or **`SMB`** **`Hotfix`** accessibility—not unrelated **`CSV`** latency—owns the outage narrative ahead of tenant-visible **`live migration`** failures.
implementation: (1) Forward **`Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/*`** channels via **`Splunk_TA_windows`** into **`patching`**; (2) ship **`HEC`** **`cau:report`**/**`cau:run`** JSON from **`Get-CauReport`**/**`Get-CauRun`** on maintenance cadences; (3) enrich **`cluster_key`** / **`cau_mode`** (**Self-Updating** vs **Remote-Updating**) via scripted tags; (4) alert on **`immediate_review`** posture rows with **`suppress`** keyed to **`MaintenanceWindow`** lookups.
visualization: (1) Timeline: **`evt`** **1001→1002** success streaks vs **1003** failures per **`cluster_key`.** (2) Table: **`run_id`**, **`report_status`**, **`current_node`**, **`plugins_seen`**, **`failure_hints`.** (3) Single-value: **`immediate_review`** posture counts trailing **24h.** (4) Heatmap: **`NodeStatusNotifications`** **`Status`** transitions (**Idle→Searching→Installing→Verifying**) per **`patch_wave`.** (5) Choropleth optional via **`CMDB`** site tags—not native **`Splunk`** unless enriched.
knownFalsePositives:
**Extended PreUpdateScript rehearsal windows** — orchestrators deliberately stretch **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`** waits past ordinary SLA bounds while scripted backups finish; **`Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin`** **`1023`** mirrors hazardous stalls though **`Invoke-CauRun`** intent stays approved—suppress rows tied to **`MaintenanceWindow`** lookups tagging **`Add-CauClusterRole`** schedules.

**Multi-reboot cumulative updates** — **`Microsoft.WindowsUpdatePlugin`** occasionally sequences **`Installing`**→**`Restarting`** twice inside one **`Get-CauReport`** boundary so **`NodeStatusNotifications`** briefly reads **`Failed`** between reboot hops—confirm **`WU`** **`KB`** ordering via **`Get-CauReport`** **`Summary`** before paging **`HotfixPlugin`** pathways.

**Administrator-cancelled Remote-Updating runs** — **`Invoke-CauRun`** **`Cancelled`** **`Status`** surfaces beside orderly **`1002`** completions—tie **`Splunk`** **`combined_status`** rows to **`Stop-CauRun`** ticket approvals before **`Severity`** escalation.

**Microsoft Defender interactive scans overlapping CAU installs** — **`Downloading`** slows while **real-time antivirus inspection** touches **`Windows`** **`Servicing`** staging folders— **`Installing`** eventually **`Succeeded`** without **`1008`** **`failure_reason`** payloads.

**Merged snapshot backlog blocking Drain** — clustered guests awaiting **`checkpoint`** merges (`Hyper-V` **`snapshot`** hygiene debt) stall **`Suspend-ClusterNode`** exactly like **`DDA`** pinning— **`1023`** clears once **`guest`** disks consolidate—not because **`Microsoft.HotfixPlugin`** **`SMB`** **`ACL`** faults appeared.

**CSV redirected-access churn during migrations** — **`Verifying`** finishes (**`evt`** **`1007`**) yet **`Perf`** **`latency`** spikes persist elsewhere— **`Splunk`** **`CAU`** posture stays **`Succeeded`** while sibling capacity dashboards blush (**avoid duplicate RCA**).

**Offline SMB Hotfix bundle swaps mid-wave** — administrators temporarily revoke **`\\share\\kb`** **`READ`** during antivirus sweeps— **`Microsoft.HotfixPlugin`** **`Summary`** **`Failed`** clears after **`icacls`** restoration without implying **`Resume-ClusterNode`** (**`1027`**) defects.
hardware: Failover Cluster nodes running Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 Datacenter or Azure Stack HCI 23H2 — Dell PowerEdge R650/R750/R760, HPE ProLiant DL380, Lenovo SR650, Cisco UCS C240, Supermicro 2029U HCI/Hyper-V certified hardware. CAU coordinator can run on the cluster itself (Self-Updating mode) or from a separate Windows admin workstation (Remote-Updating mode).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Deploy Splunk Universal Forwarder plus Splunk_TA_windows (Splunkbase 742) on each Failover Cluster node running Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 Datacenter or Azure Stack HCI 23H2, indexing `Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin`, `Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating-Management/Admin`, and `Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational` into `patching` so EventCode/Message pairs survive TA parsing for correlation with `Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain` / `Resume-ClusterNode` sequencing.
• Install ClusterAwareUpdating PowerShell module on collector hosts; validate Get-CauRun / Get-CauReport availability. Self-Updating installs rely on `Add-CauClusterRole` / `Set-CauClusterRole` schedules; Remote-Updating captures `Invoke-CauRun` / `Invoke-CauScan` sessions from hardened admin workstations obeying CredSSP/Kerberos delegation policy.
• Provision HTTP Event Collector endpoints for `cau:report` (`Get-CauReport -ClusterName … -Last 10 | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10`) and `cau:run` (`Get-CauRun -ClusterName … | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10`), emitting `RunId`, `Status`, `CurrentNodeName`, `NodeStatusNotifications`, `RunPlugin` snapshots every five minutes during patch waves.
• Ensure UNC targets referenced by `Set-CauClusterRole -PreUpdateScript` / `-PostUpdateScript` grant CNO identity read/execute rights across every node; misconfigured SMB ACLs mimic `Microsoft.HotfixPlugin` failures even when `Microsoft.WindowsUpdatePlugin` succeeds.

Step 1 — Configure ingestion paths
(1) Enable WinEventLog stanzas exactly as shipped in Splunk docs for `Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin` (IDs 1001–1008, 1011–1016, 1023, 1027) plus `Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating-Management/Admin` for cluster-role edits (Add-CauClusterRole, role toggles).
(2) Forward `Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational` alongside CAU feeds so `Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain` timeouts (1023) can be traced next to `Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole` cancellations logged outside CAU Admin text.
(3) Schedule scripted `Get-CauReport` pushes daily (hourly near Patch Tuesday) into `cau:report` preserving **`Summary`**, **`NodeUpdateState[]`**, **`WorkflowConfiguration`**, **`RunPluginConfigs`** fields—offline auditors rely on historical **`Summary.failedNodes`** counts even when WinEvent forwarding briefly pauses.
(4) Poll **`Get-CauRun`** during maintenance windows into **`cau:run`** to retain **`NodeStatusNotifications`** transitions (Idle→Searching→Downloading→Staging→Installing→Restarting→WaitingForRestart→Verifying→Succeeded/Failed) plus **`UpdateTitle`** strings tied to **`Microsoft.Update`** selections.
(5) Tag each payload with **`cau_mode`**=`SelfUpdating` versus `RemoteUpdating`, sourced from whether **`Invoke-CauRun`** originated interactively versus **`Enable-CauClusterRole`** orchestration—helps suppress benign Operator-cancelled **`Cancelled`** statuses.

Step 2 — Build posture SPL and macros
Normalize cmdlet JSON keys (`Summary_Status`, `RunPlugin`, etc.) via **`spath`** once sample payloads confirm dotted naming in your exporter version. Publish macros **`cau_evt_codes`** listing **1003**, **1008**, **1023**, **1027** so alerts reuse constants without typo drift across environments.

```spl
(index=patching OR index=windows OR index=wineventlog OR index=azure_stack_hci)
(sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin" OR sourcetype="cau:report" OR sourcetype="cau:run")
earliest=-24h
| eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventCode,event_id,EventID,""))
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<fail_blob>failure_reason|FailureReason)[=:]\s*(?<reason_tail>[^\|\r\n]+)"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<err_tail>error_id|ErrorId)[=:]\s*(?<err_code>[^\|\r\n]+)"
| eval run_status=upper(trim(coalesce(Status,"")))
| eval json_Status=upper(trim(coalesce(Summary_Status,report_Status,"")))
| eval combined_status=coalesce(run_status,json_Status)
| eval plugin_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(RunPlugin,run_plugin,"")))
| eval urgent=case(
    evt IN ("1003","1008","1023","1027"), 3,
    combined_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED"), 3,
    run_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED"), 3,
    match(lower(_raw), "(?i)suspend-clusternode\s+-drain.*timeout"), 3,
    match(lower(_raw), "(?i)resume-clusternode.*fail"), 3,
    true(),1)
| where urgent=3 OR combined_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED") OR (evt IN ("1011","1012","1015","1016") AND match(lower(_raw),"fail"))
| eval cluster_key=upper(trim(coalesce(ClusterName,cluster_name,fq_cluster,ComputerName)))
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(evt) as last_evt,
       latest(combined_status) as report_status,
       latest(CurrentNodeName) as current_node,
       latest(RunId) as run_id,
       values(plugin_hint) as plugins_seen,
       values(reason_tail) as failure_hints,
       values(err_code) as error_hints,
       dc(host) as reporting_hosts
  by cluster_key
| eval posture=case(report_status IN ("FAILED","CANCELLED") OR last_evt IN ("1003","1008","1023","1027"),"immediate_review",true(),"monitor")
| sort -last_seen
| table cluster_key, posture, last_evt, report_status, current_node, run_id, plugins_seen, failure_hints, error_hints, reporting_hosts, last_seen
```

Document macro ownership inside **`macros/README`** so rotating SMEs remember **`Summary_Status`** aliases track flattened **`Get-CauReport`** exports versus nested **`Summary.Status`** paths after **`spath`** transforms.

Step 3 — Validate telemetry vs consoles
(1) Compare **`RunId`** values between **`Get-CauRun`** live output and Splunk **`cau:run`** rows during rehearsal—timestamp skew should remain below ingestion latency budgets (< five minutes).
(2) Replay **`Invoke-CauScan`** dry-run events and verify **`Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin`** **1006**/**1007** sequences precede **`Get-CauReport`** **`Summary.successfulNodes`** increments—disconnect indicates **`WinEventLog`** backlog risk on saturated hosts.
(3) Capture **`Save-CauDebugTrace`** bundles whenever **`evt`** **1023** fires during **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`**—Splunk proves correlation windows while traces expose **`Hyper-V`** **`Cannot migrate`** causes (**GPU-passthrough**, **snapshot backlog**, anti-affinity constraints).

Step 4 — Operationalise alerting and dashboards
(1) Alert when **`posture=immediate_review`** with suppression keyed on **`cluster_key` + `run_id`** plus CMDB **`MaintenanceWindow`** IDs tied to **`Invoke-CauRun`** approvals.
(2) Dashboard lane showing **`plugins_seen`** counts distinguishing **`microsoft.windowsupdateplugin`** versus **`microsoft.hotfixplugin`**—SMB **`Hotfix`** regressions manifest differently than **`WU`** catalog outages.
(3) Weekly hygiene report exporting **`last_seen`** drift versus contractual patch-window deadlines—pairs **`Change`** ticketing references.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting / misleading modes

• **`NodeStatusNotifications.Status`=`Searching`** indefinitely — BITS/WSUS backlog upstream of **`Microsoft.WindowsUpdatePlugin`**; confirm **`Invoke-CauScan`** succeeds interactively before blaming **`ClusterAwareUpdating`** coordinator binaries.

• **`Microsoft.HotfixPlugin`** UNC **`Access denied`** — **`Summary`**/`NodeUpdateState[]` fails yet **`Suspend-ClusterNode`** never enters **`Drain`**—repair **`\\fs01\\hotfixes`** ACL for **CNO** plus Cluster Nodes group before **`Resume-ClusterNode`** retries (**1027**) cascade.

• **`Suspend-ClusterNode -Drain`** timeout (**1023**) with **GPU** Discrete Device Assignment guests — **`Move-ClusterVirtualMachineRole`** cannot relocate VMs relying on **DDA** passthrough—live migrate impossible until shutdown.

• Anti-affinity constraints on **two-node** clusters — **`Waiting`** drains exceed SLA because conflicting VMs cannot separate— **`Get-ClusterGroup`** placement proves workload intent mismatch versus **`CAU`** fault.

• **`Replica`** synchronization halted — clustered VMs stall migration identical to **`Drain`** hangs though **`Resume-ClusterNode`** healthy—repair **`Replica`** topology before **`Invoke-CauRun`** rerun.

• **`Snapshots`** awaiting merge — **`Hyper-V`** **`Cannot migrate`** precedes **1023** until checkpoints consolidate—Splunk regex hints **`snapshot`** keywords inside **`failure_hints`**.

• **`Clustered Scale-Out File Server`** roles pinning **`IP Address`** dependencies — **`Drain`** hangs waiting application teardown— correlate **`FailoverClustering`** Operational IDs alongside **`CAU`** lifecycle markers.

• **`PostUpdateScript`** exit mismatch — **1016** might still note completion although **`stderr`** logged failures— **`Save-CauDebugTrace`** mandatory when Splunk sees **`fail`** tokens inside **`PostUpdateScript`** streams only.

• **`Microsoft Defender`** scanning **`Servicing`** caches — lengthen **`Downloading`**/`Installing` overlaps mistaken as **`Microsoft.WindowsUpdatePlugin`** timeouts unless Defender exclusions validated.

• **`Invoke-CauRun`** **`Cancelled`** operator stops (`Status=Canceled`) — benign daylight cancellations deserve **`MaintenanceWindow`** suppression unlike **`evt`** **1003** abnormal exits.

• **`CSV redirected-access`** spikes — **`Verifying`** succeeds per **`evt`** **1007** yet capacity dashboards flare—avoid duplicate RCA versus sibling Storage Spaces UC families.

• **`Resume-ClusterNode`** faults (**1027**) — isolate NIC teaming resets versus **`Hotfix`** binary faults before swapping **`Microsoft.HotfixPlugin`** SMB payloads during RCA reviews.
grandmaExplanation: We line up rolling restart rounds across clustered machines so uneven patch footing and stuck drain moments surface early before everyday apps hitch when picky workloads refuse to slide elsewhere.
